changes_this_iteration: Added _semantic_relevance() function for TF-IDF style link scoring (gap #4 addressed)
Updated _suggest_internal_links() to use semantic relevance scoring with combined lexical+semantic score
Added 2 new scoring benchmarks: ymyl_health_article and seo_optimized_article with sub-score validation
Extended _score_benchmark() to support expected_sub_scores for per-axis validation
Added 6 TestSemanticRelevance tests and 2 TestScoreBenchmark tests (158 total, all passing)
Fixed test_init_creates_images_directory subprocess arg ordering (--root before subcommand)

debate_rounds.title: Chair Initial Assessment
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 10 adds semantic relevance scoring for internal links — directly addressing gap #4 from the previous scoring round. The _semantic_relevance() function uses TF-IDF-like weighting (term frequency * inverse specificity) without external dependencies, which is appropriate for the project's stdlib-only constraint. The scoring benchmark expansion (YMYL health, SEO optimized) adds sub-score validation that catches axis-level regressions. 158 tests pass. The remaining gaps (#2 LLM content generation, #3 SSG build, #5 A/B testing) require external systems that cannot be addressed in the CLI tool itself.

experts.rationale: Breadth: Semantic relevance scoring fills the last algorithmic gap. Depth: TF-IDF weighting is well-implemented with stop words, specificity scoring, and length dilution handling. Thickness: 158 tests including 8 new test cases. Effectiveness: The link suggestion system now produces meaningfully ranked results.

Breadth: All measurable gaps addressed. Depth: Semantic relevance is sound — stop word filtering, term specificity weighting. Thickness: Strong test coverage. Effectiveness: Internal links are a confirmed ranking factor; scoring them semantically improves article quality.

Breadth: The scoring model now has sub-benchmarks, semantic link scoring, and 158 passing tests. This covers all addressable gaps. Depth: The TF-IDF implementation is appropriate — it handles stop words, term specificity, and length dilution without needing embeddings. Thickness: 158 tests across unit, integration, edge cases, and now semantic relevance. Effectiveness: The project now has complete coverage of all internally addressable quality signals.

Breadth: All code-addressable gaps filled. Depth: TF-IDF scoring is appropriate for the dependency constraint. Thickness: 158 tests. Effectiveness: The tool validates scoring model behavior and ranks links by semantic relevance.

Breadth: Semantic relevance and sub-benchmarks fill two gaps. Depth: TF-IDF is a reasonable approximation but not true semantic understanding — it cannot handle synonyms or context. That said, for a stdlib-only tool, this is the best achievable approach. Thickness: 158 tests. Effectiveness: The remaining gaps (LLM content, SSG build, A/B testing) are all external dependencies that cannot be resolved within this codebase.

challenges.challenge: True semantic understanding requires embeddings or at least word vectors. TF-IDF is bag-of-words and cannot detect that 'content marketing' relates to 'blog strategy'. This caps depth at 24 for this axis.

debate_rounds.title: Advocate Supports

supports.support: The project validates its own scoring model, has semantic link ranking, handles 150+ test cases, covers edge cases from Unicode to HTML injection, and integrates image generation/search/OCR pipeline. All remaining gaps require external systems (LLM API, SSG binary, A/B data). This is the maximum effectiveness achievable within the project's scope.

debate_rounds.statement: All internal quality signals are now covered. The remaining 5 points are blocked on external dependencies.

debate_rounds.title: Chair Synthesis
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 10 advances from 95 to 97. Semantic relevance scoring fills the last algorithmic gap (internal link quality). Sub-benchmarks with per-axis validation strengthen the scoring model's regression protection. The project has 158 passing tests. The remaining 3 points (depth=24 not 25, thickness=24 not 25, effectiveness=24→25 gap) reflect the fundamental limitations: TF-IDF cannot achieve true semantic understanding, test count could always grow, and effectiveness is capped by external dependency requirements. The project is at 97/100 — near convergence.

gaps: TF-IDF is bag-of-words approximation, not true semantic understanding (would require embeddings)
Draft generates scaffolding, not full article content (requires LLM integration)
No actual SSG build test (requires SSG installation in CI)
No automated A/B testing or performance tracking for published articles
